title: Executing the Compile-Run-Fix Loop
concept: Running code, capturing syntax errors, and piping them back to the agent as observations.

exercises.prompt: What is the main purpose of a code sandbox?

options.text: To run code in an isolated environment safely.
To store all computer files permanently.

exercises.explain: A code sandbox allows us to run code safely without risking changes to our main system.

cards.title: Running Code
cards.body: When we run code, we see what it does in real time. Think of it like following a recipe to see how a dish turns out.

cards.title: Syntax Errors
cards.body: Syntax errors are mistakes in the code that prevent it from running properly. It’s like spelling errors in a sentence.

cards.title: Compile-Run-Fix Loop
cards.body: This loop is like a cycle: you bake a cake (run code), check for mistakes (capture errors), then fix the recipe (correct code). Repeat until it’s perfect!

cards.title: Example of Running Code
cards.body: If we write "print('Hello!')" and run it, we expect to see "Hello!" in the output. If there is an error, we’ll know something is wrong.

cards.title: Common Myth: All Errors Are Bad
cards.body: Not all errors are terrible! Errors help us learn and improve our coding skills.

cards.title: Compile-Run-Fix Cycle
cards.body: This cycle helps us develop better code by ensuring we fix errors gradually.

exercises.prompt: What should you do after running code and finding a syntax error?

options.text: Ignore it and run the code again.
Write down the error and fix it before running again.
Change the language of the code completely.
Ask someone else to run the code for you.

exercises.explain: Writing down the syntax error allows you to understand what went wrong and fix it before trying again.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Running code can sometimes lead to more errors that need to be addressed.
exercises.explain: True! Running code can reveal errors that we don’t see while just writing it.

exercises.prompt: The process of fixing errors after running code is called the _____ loop.
exercises.sentence: The process of fixing errors after running code is called the Compile-Run-Fix loop.
exercises.explain: The Compile-Run-Fix loop is essential for developing functional code.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following is a step in the Compile-Run-Fix cycle?

options.text: Only writing code without running it.
Running code to check for errors.
Only fixing code without running it.

exercises.explain: Running code is a crucial step in the Compile-Run-Fix cycle as it allows us to see errors in action.

exercises.prompt: What should you do if you encounter a syntax error after running your code?

options.text: Leave it and start a new project.
Make note of the error and fix it before running the code again.
Run the code again multiple times hoping it fixes itself.
Ask your friend to run it instead.

exercises.explain: The best approach is to note the syntax error, fix it, and then rerun the code to ensure it works. This process builds your coding skills!
